questions: For a video clip inside a pinned shot group that has an asset key but no real generation row, should double-click open the MediaLightbox or the shot video modal? The current test name and assertion disagree, so this changes resolver priority.

When a raw uploaded asset opens in MediaLightbox, should it be treated as strictly view/download-only, or should any generation-oriented actions remain available if they can work without a backing generation?
Is the waveform issue expected to stay limited to source-URL selection, or do you also want signed/expired URL fetch behavior in `useWaveformData` addressed in this same cleanup?

success_criteria.criterion: All video-editor double-click entry points use one shared clip-open decision path, so timeline clips and preview overlays no longer implement divergent routing rules.
The shared clip-open logic opens MediaLightbox only for assets that are explicitly lightbox-openable, and otherwise falls back to the video modal or no-op according to the agreed precedence.
Generation-backed and raw assets share the same lightbox-openable contract, with focused tests covering both paths.
Any waveform fix stays isolated to waveform URL/helper code and does not add clip-open branching or lightbox-specific fallback logic.
Focused tests for routing, lightbox media building, and any touched waveform behavior pass, and `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
No broad MediaLightbox architecture rewrite is introduced; the change remains a local cleanup in the video-editor routing/provider surfaces plus tests.
Manual smoke test confirms that double-clicking a generation-backed clip, a raw uploaded clip, and a pinned-group final-video clip each opens the intended destination.

assumptions: Proceed with the smallest local cleanup rather than a MediaLightbox or timeline architecture rewrite.
Until clarified, prefer this routing rule: openable asset wins and opens MediaLightbox; the video modal is only a fallback when there is no lightbox-openable asset for the clip.
Raw uploaded image/video assets with a resolvable URL should remain lightbox-openable, but this change should not invent a new generation lookup path for them.
The provider should keep owning lightbox state and fallback-media construction, while clip-level target resolution should move to a shared pure helper.
Waveform work should remain a separate concern and only change if the current bug is reproducibly tied to URL selection or decoding in the existing waveform path.
